message.content:
Explore the Belt monorepo structure thoroughly. I need to understand:

1. All package.json files - their names, versions, dependencies, scripts, and NPM publishing configuration (main, types, exports, files, publishConfig, etc.)
2. The monorepo root configuration (pnpm-workspace.yaml, turbo.json)
3. Build output structure - what's in dist folders after build
4. Current tsconfig settings for each package

Focus on identifying what's missing or incorrect for NPM publishing. Check if packages have proper entry points, type declarations, and peer dependencies configured correctly.

Be very thorough - this is for NPM publishing preparation.

{
  "name": "belt",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "description": "Developer toolkit - command palette, quality tools, and state inspector",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt"
  },
  "author": "Antonio Presto <antoniopresto@gmail.com>",
  "license": "MIT",
  "packageManager": "pnpm@9.14.2",
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=20.0.0"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "build": "turbo run build",
    "dev": "turbo run dev",
    "dev:cli": "turbo run dev --filter=@belt/cli",
    "dev:desktop": "turbo run dev --filter=@belt/desktop",
    "lint": "biome check .",
    "lint:fix": "biome check --write .",
    "format": "biome format --write .",
    "test": "turbo run test",
    "typecheck": "turbo run typecheck",
    "clean": "turbo run clean && rm -rf node_modules"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@biomejs/biome": "^2.3.8",
    "@types/node": "^22.0.0",
    "turbo": "^2.3.0",
    "typescript": "^5.9.3"
  }
}
